content.description: A unique fact is: Honeybees communicate the location of food sources to their hive-mates through a 'waggle dance' — the angle of the dance relative to the sun indicates direction, while the duration indicates distance.

metadata.reasoning: Well-established ethological research by Karl von Frisch (Nobel Prize 1973). The waggle dance is one of the most studied non-human communication systems.

trajectory.detail: Identified lesser-known aspects of honeybee behaviour beyond honey production
Selected waggle dance as it conveys precise quantitative information, surprising for insects
Verified Nobel Prize attribution and mechanism accuracy
